board.designation: GH-DB — Gate-house sub-DB, Nairobi industrial light-engineering workshop
board.manufacturer_class: KS Annex E (BS EN 61439-3 Distribution Board)

incoming_supply.fed_from: MSP-100 C08 (40A MCB Type B + 30mA Type A RCD, 60m submain)

selectivity_results.upstream: MSP-100 C08 (40A MCB Type B + 30mA Type A RCD)
selectivity_results._note: Upstream 40A MCB selectively protects against 16A and smaller final-circuit MCBs at GH-DB (current ratio ≥2.5:1). RCD selectivity NOT achieved: upstream 30mA Type A at MSP-100 C08 and downstream 30mA Type A on C02/C03 are both 30mA — series 30mA pairs are time-coincident (KS 1700 §314 requires staged sensitivities for true selectivity). Coordination accepted because the upstream RCD also serves only the gate house; an earth fault tripping both is operationally acceptable for a small isolated load.

assumptions: KS 1700:2018 Annex E adopts BS EN 61439-3 for distribution boards; KS Annex E adopts BS Table 41.2 disconnection-time tables verbatim.
KS 1700:2018 §411.3.3 universal socket-RCD policy: 30mA Type A applied on C02 (socket) and C03 (comms outlet — small DC content from CCTV switching supplies).
Estimated PFC at GH-DB ≈ 3.0 kA after the 60m submain impedance from 9.8 kA declared at MSP-100; 6 kA Icn on the GH-DB MCBs provides 100% headroom.
Single-phase 240V intake — gate house is a small isolated load best fed as single-phase to simplify isolation and minimise neutral imbalance at MSP-100.

drawing_notes: GH-DB located inside the gate-house plant cupboard at the site perimeter.
Fed from MSP-100 C08 via 60m 10mm² 4-core SWA PVC Cu submain.
IP54 enclosure rating suits semi-outdoor gate-house cupboard.
